$description: Tier 1 — option tokens (constitution §22.1): raw scales with no meaning attached. Semantic roles alias into this tier; components never consume it directly (sanctioned exception: the space scale, §8). The engineered gray ramps and named hue scales land here in the palette round — their absence is the known hole this structure exists to receive.

color.$description: The named hue scales (ratified 2026-07-13, replacing the parametric tag formula). Each hue is INDIVIDUALLY placed — per-hue L/C, no formula. Solids are world-independent (tag identity stable across themes); on-solid ink is a property of the HUE (amber + lime are the bright inversion set: dark text, the Radix lesson). World-varying steps (tint, tint-ink, line) live in ../worlds/hues-{light,dark}.tokens.json. Interaction states for chips: derived per §4 when a hoverable chip component exists — not pre-authored.

alx.note: anchored in the Vercel-blue neighborhood by taste (2026-07-12)

space.$description: Quantum multiples (§8): the name IS the multiple count (space.3 = 3 × 4px). The one primitive components consume directly, for all gaps/padding/margins. 2px is NOT a step — sub-quantum values are optical detail (see stroke), not spacing.

font.$description: The cast (§9). All bundled; identical on every platform.

alx.role: joy register ONLY (§16); banned from working chrome
dot voice in type; rarest register (§16)

type-scale.$description: The UI scale as typography composites (§9): size + line-height + tracking are ONE token so they can never drift apart. Weight here is the regular default; semantic type ROLES (concept → composite + weight + ink, the §30.4 pinning work) land in semantic.tokens.json in the type round. House deviations from DTCG, both deliberate: lineHeight is a px dimension (px-locked chrome §8; the spec's unitless number invites rounding drift) and letterSpacing is an em string (spec wants px/rem; note the values are sub-pixel at 1× — §8's garnish clause — the type round decides whether tracking survives at all).

alx.role: display tier (ratified 2026-07-13): view-level chrome only, never body UI
display tier (ratified 2026-07-13): the largest chrome voice

alx.pin: ratified 2026-07-13 as the micro voice; below the 11px floor hypothesis — NON-ESSENTIAL text only (cell indexes, keyboard hints, timestamp garnish); 1×/Windows check pending (§28)
hypothesis (§30.4) — NOTE §9 says section heads are bold at BODY size; whether a 13px rung survives the type round is open

duration.$description: §26: register shifts ≤ fast; transients base; the one hero transition slow.

stroke.$description: Line weights (§3, §8): deliberately NOT on the 4px grid — the quantum governs layout, never stroke width.

alx.role: emphasis stroke: hover underline, focus ring
